page.title: Constitution Day Weekend in Marbella: 6th of December Yacht Itinerary

data.meta_description: Plan your Constitution Day Marbella yacht charter for the 6th of December. Explore Puerto Banús, Cabopino & Estepona on a luxury boat from €749/2h.
data.summary: A detailed guide to chartering a yacht in Marbella for Constitution Day weekend (6th December), with recommended itineraries, pricing from €749/2h, and tips for making the most of the long holiday.
key_facts: Constitution Day (6th December) is a Spanish national holiday, ideal for a long weekend yacht charter.
Charters depart from Puerto Banús; boats range from 6.4m to 29m, accommodating 5–12 guests.
Tier A boats (Astondoa 40, Azimut 39) from €749/2h to €2,299/8h, all inclusive.
Tier B boats (Mangusta 80) from €4,719/4h, includes a free jet ski.
Inclusions: skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, insurance, and 21% VAT.
December sea temps average 16°C; pack a jacket and sunscreen.
Popular stops: Cala del Faro, Cala Cortés, and Marbella Marina.
Book early – Constitution Day weekend is popular with locals and tourists.

faq.q: What is Constitution Day in Spain?
faq.a: Constitution Day (Día de la Constitución) is a national public holiday on 6th December, commemorating the 1978 Spanish Constitution. It often creates a long weekend, making it a popular time for leisure activities like yacht charters in Marbella.

faq.q: Can I charter a yacht on Constitution Day itself?
faq.a: Yes, most charters operate on 6th December, but availability is limited. We recommend booking at least 2–3 weeks in advance. All our boats depart from Puerto Banús, with prices starting at €749 for 2 hours on Tier A boats.

faq.q: What is the weather like in Marbella on 6th December?
faq.a: December in Marbella is mild, with daytime highs around 17–19°C and sea temperatures near 16°C. Expect sunny intervals and occasional clouds. A light jacket is advisable, but the sun deck can still be pleasant.

faq.q: Which yacht is best for a small group on Constitution Day weekend?
faq.a: For a group of 5–6, the Speedboat (€749/2h) or Dubhe (€749/2h) are great value. For up to 9 guests, the Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39 (both Tier A, €749/2h) offer comfort and style. Larger groups up to 12 can book the Mangusta 80 (Tier B, from €4,719/4h).

faq.q: What is included in the charter price?
faq.a: Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21% VAT). No hidden costs.

faq.q: Where can we sail to from Puerto Banús in December?
faq.a: Popular December itineraries include cruising to Cabopino (20 min east), Marbella Marina (15 min west), or Cala del Faro (30 min west). Cala Cortés is also a calm anchorage. The sea is usually calm, but always check conditions with your skipper.

faq.q: Is it safe to swim in December?
faq.a: Swimming is possible, but the water is cool at 16°C. Some guests enjoy a quick dip, but it's not for everyone. The boat's heated cabin or flybridge is a cosy alternative.

faq.q: How do I book a yacht for Constitution Day weekend?
faq.a: Book directly via our website or WhatsApp. For Tier A boats, you can select a date and time online. For Tier B and other vessels, contact us for availability and pricing. Early booking is strongly advised.

data.body_html:
Constitution Day (Día de la Constitución) on 6th December is one of Spain's most significant national holidays, and in Marbella it means a long weekend of sunshine, sea, and celebration. A Constitution Day Marbella yacht charter offers a unique way to enjoy the holiday, combining luxury with the Costa del Sol's stunning coastline. Whether you're a local or a visitor, spending the day on the water is an experience you won't forget. Our fleet departs from Puerto Banús, and with prices starting at €749 for two hours, there's a boat for every group.

Why Charter a Yacht on Constitution Day Weekend?

6th December often falls on a Thursday or Friday, creating a puente (bridge) weekend. The weather in Marbella remains mild, with average highs of 18°C and blue skies. The Mediterranean is calm, and the marinas are less crowded than in summer. A yacht charter lets you escape the holiday bustle and enjoy the coast from a private perspective. With all-inclusive pricing—skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, and VAT—you can relax without worrying about extras.

Our Fleet for Constitution Day Charters

We offer a range of boats to suit different group sizes and budgets. All depart from Puerto Banús, Marbella's iconic marina. Here's a quick overview:

  • Tier A (from €749/2h to €2,299/8h): Astondoa 40 (12.5m, 9 pax) and Azimut 39 (12.5m, 11 pax). Perfect for families or small groups.
  • Tier B (from €4,719/4h): Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax) in grey, white, or standard. Includes a free jet ski.
  • Other boats (pricing on request): Azimut 58 Flybridge (17.6m, 12 pax), Ferretti 94 (29m, 12 pax), Pershing 46 (14m, 10 pax), and more.

For a full list, visit our boat rental page.

Recommended Itinerary for 6th December

Start your day at Puerto Banús, boarding at 10:00 or 11:00. Head east towards Cabopino (20 minutes), a charming marina with a sandy beach and dunes. From there, cruise to Marbella Marina (15 minutes) for views of La Concha mountain. Continue west to Cala del Faro, a secluded cove near the lighthouse, perfect for anchoring and enjoying your included cava. For a longer trip, sail to Estepona (45 minutes west) or Sotogrande (1 hour west). Your skipper will tailor the route to your preferences and sea conditions.

What's Included in Your Charter

All our charters are all-inclusive, so you don't need to worry about a thing:

  • Licensed skipper (English and Spanish speaking)
  • Fuel for the entire trip
  • Water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, and cava
  • Light snacks (olives, crisps, nuts)
  • Insurance and safety equipment
  • Spanish IVA (21% VAT)

No hidden fees. Just show up and enjoy.

December Weather and What to Wear

Marbella in December is mild but can be breezy. Daytime temperatures range from 15°C to 20°C. Sea temperature is around 16°C. We recommend bringing a light jacket or sweater, sunglasses, and sunscreen (the sun is still strong). Comfortable shoes with non-slip soles are ideal for the deck. If you plan to swim, a wetsuit or rash guard is advisable.

Booking Tips for Constitution Day Weekend

Constitution Day is a popular holiday, so book early. Tier A boats can be booked online directly. For Tier B and other vessels, contact us via WhatsApp for availability and pricing. We recommend a 4-hour charter (€1,499 on Tier A) to make the most of the day, or a full 8-hour charter (€2,299) for a complete coastal exploration. Larger groups should consider the Mangusta 80 for space and the free jet ski.

Frequently Asked Questions

What is Constitution Day in Spain?

Constitution Day (Día de la Constitución) is a national public holiday on 6th December, commemorating the 1978 Spanish Constitution. It often creates a long weekend, making it a popular time for leisure activities like yacht charters in Marbella.

Can I charter a yacht on Constitution Day itself?

Yes, most charters operate on 6th December, but availability is limited. We recommend booking at least 2–3 weeks in advance. All our boats depart from Puerto Banús, with prices starting at €749 for 2 hours on Tier A boats.

What is the weather like in Marbella on 6th December?

December in Marbella is mild, with daytime highs around 17–19°C and sea temperatures near 16°C. Expect sunny intervals and occasional clouds. A light jacket is advisable, but the sun deck can still be pleasant.

Which yacht is best for a small group on Constitution Day weekend?

For a group of 5–6, the Speedboat (€749/2h) or Dubhe (€749/2h) are great value. For up to 9 guests, the Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39 (both Tier A, €749/2h) offer comfort and style. Larger groups up to 12 can book the Mangusta 80 (Tier B, from €4,719/4h).

What is included in the charter price?

Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21% VAT). No hidden costs.

Where can we sail to from Puerto Banús in December?

Popular December itineraries include cruising to Cabopino (20 min east), Marbella Marina (15 min west), or Cala del Faro (30 min west). Cala Cortés is also a calm anchorage. The sea is usually calm, but always check conditions with your skipper.

Is it safe to swim in December?

Swimming is possible, but the water is cool at 16°C. Some guests enjoy a quick dip, but it's not for everyone. The boat's heated cabin or flybridge is a cosy alternative.

How do I book a yacht for Constitution Day weekend?

Book directly via our website or WhatsApp. For Tier A boats, you can select a date and time online. For Tier B and other vessels, contact us for availability and pricing. Early booking is strongly advised.
